title: Ayatul Kursi (The Throne Verse)
translation: In the Name of Allah, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful. Allah! There is no deity except Him, the Ever-Living, the Sustainer of existence. Neither drowsiness overtakes Him nor sleep. To Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. Who is it that can intercede with Him except by His permission? He knows what is before them and what will be after them, and they encompass not a thing of His knowledge except for what He wills. His Kursi extends over the heavens and the earth, and their preservation tires Him not. And He is the Most High, the Most Great.

title: Hadith Dua: Refuge in Allah's Perfect Words
translation: I seek refuge in the perfect words of Allah from the evil of what He has created.

title: Hadith Dua: Nothing Can Harm in Allah's Name
reference: Al-Adab Al-Mufrad 660 | Musnad Ahmad 446 | Hisnul Muslim 86
translation: In the Name of Allah, with Whose Name nothing on earth or in heaven can cause harm, and He is the All-Hearing, the All-Knowing.

title: Surah Al-Ikhlas
translation: In the Name of Allah, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful. 1. Say, He is Allah, [who is] One. 2. Allah, the Eternal Refuge. 3. He neither begets nor is born. 4. Nor is there to Him any equivalent.

title: Surah Al-Falaq
translation: In the Name of Allah, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful. 1. Say, I seek refuge in the Lord of daybreak. 2. From the evil of that which He created. 3. And from the evil of darkness when it settles. 4. And from the evil of the blowers in knots. 5. And from the evil of an envier when he envies.

title: Surah An-Nas
translation: In the Name of Allah, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful. 1. Say, I seek refuge in the Lord of mankind. 2. The Sovereign of mankind. 3. The God of mankind. 4. From the evil of the retreating whisperer. 5. Who whispers [evil] into the breasts of mankind. 6. From among the jinn and mankind.

title: Protection from the Influence of Shaytan
translation: There is no true god but Allah alone, with no partner. His is the dominion and His is all praise, and He is over all things Omnipotent.

title: Part of Surah At-Tawbah, Verse 129
translation: Allah is sufficient for me; there is no true god except Him. I have placed my trust in Him, and He is the Lord of the Mighty Throne.

title: Dua for Protection from Shaytan
translation: I seek refuge in Allah, the All-Hearing, the All-Knowing, from the accursed Shaytan.

title: Dua Against the Evil Eye
translation: O Allah, remove the harm, the heat, the cold, and the harsh effects of the evil eye.

title: Protection from Black Magic
translation: I seek refuge in the perfect words of Allah, which no good or evil person can overstep, from the evil of what He has created, made, and spread abroad, and in all of His beautiful names, those I know and those I do not know.

title: Protection from Magic and Black Magic
translation: We have entered the morning/evening, and with it all dominion belongs to Allah, and all praise is for Allah. I seek refuge in Allah from the evil of what He has created, the evil of what spreads abroad, and the evil of Shaytan and his causing others to associate partners with Allah.

title: Dua at a Time of Danger or Fear
translation: O Allah, we place You before us against our enemies, and we seek refuge in You from their evil.

title: Protection from Illness (Ruqyah)
reference: Sahih Muslim 5829, narrated by Abu Sa'id
translation: In the Name of Allah I recite over you, protecting you from everything that may harm you, from the evil of every soul or envious eye. May Allah heal you. In the Name of Allah I recite over you.

title: Hadith Dua for Well-Being
translation: O Allah, grant my body well-being. O Allah, grant my hearing well-being. O Allah, grant my sight well-being. There is no true god but You.

title: Dua for Steadfastness in Faith
translation: O Turner of the hearts, keep my heart firm upon Your religion.

title: Dua for Relief from Sorrow and Distress
translation: O Ever-Living, O Self-Sustaining Sustainer, by Your mercy I seek help.

title: Dua for Protection from Hellfire
translation: O Allah, save me from the Fire.

title: Dua for Well-Being in This World and the Hereafter
translation: O Allah, I ask You for well-being (afiyah) in this world and the Hereafter.

title: Protection from Four Things
translation: O Allah, I seek refuge in You from knowledge that does not benefit, from a heart that does not fear You, from a soul that is never satisfied, and from a supplication that is not answered.

title: Comprehensive Dua for Well-Being and Protection
reference: Sunan Ibn Majah 3871 | Sunan Abi Dawud 5074
translation: O Allah, I ask You for forgiveness and well-being in this world and the Hereafter. O Allah, I ask You for forgiveness and well-being in my religion, my worldly affairs, my family, and my wealth. O Allah, conceal my faults and calm my fears. O Allah, protect me from before me, from behind me, from my right, from my left, and from above me, and I seek refuge in Your greatness from being taken unaware from beneath me.

title: The Three Tasbih
translation: Glory be to Allah (SubhanAllah). All praise is for Allah (Alhamdulillah). Allah is the Greatest (Allahu Akbar).

title: Dua for Forgiveness of Sins
translation: I glorify Allah with all praise due to Him.
